nodes.rationale: IPSG SK473 6W powered speaker, SKU 593038, gutted for its PAM8403 board + 2 KHD drivers; 6 used = 12 channels, the binding constraint. Each box's single 3.5 mm jack is stereo = 2 independent channels (L=driver A, R=driver B); USB is shared 5 V power only.

nodes.label: Raspberry Pi 5 (owned, cut from critical path)
nodes.rationale: Raspberry Pi 5 8GB, SKU 635649, owned but cut from the critical path — the laptop is the brain. ESP32 (Elegoo, 5 owned) is not in the audio path, sensor work only.
nodes.norm_label: raspberry pi 5 (owned, cut from critical path)

nodes.label: Hardware render chain (laptop -> Vantec -> SK473 -> KHD drivers)
nodes.rationale: Laptop --USB data--> 2x Vantec NBA-200U (USB digital -> 8 analog ch each) --3.5mm plug--> 6x SK473 PAM8403 amp boards --18 AWG speaker wire soldered to output pads--> 12x KHD 3 Ohm/5 W drivers (6 back strings + 6 torso fret-zones).
nodes.norm_label: hardware render chain (laptop -> vantec -> sk473 -> khd drivers)

nodes.label: Connector cheat-sheet + bounded power isolation
nodes.rationale: The amp is the translator: 3.5 mm line-level in -> amplified power out on bare wire. Bare wire never touches the Vantec. The 3.5 mm cable shares signal ground but carries only line-level current and the amp's 5 V rail is isolated, so amp power cannot flow back into the laptop — bounded isolation, not total absence of any DC path.

nodes.label: Job 1 — gut an SK473 into a bare amp brick
nodes.norm_label: job 1 — gut an sk473 into a bare amp brick

nodes.label: 6 back string sites (high E top -> low E bottom)
nodes.rationale: 6 strings rendered as a vertical back column mirroring the guitar (high E up, low E down) for intuitiveness; channels 1-6 (back_high_e, B, G, D, A, back_low_e).
nodes.norm_label: 6 back string sites (high e top -> low e bottom)

nodes.label: 6 torso fret-zones z1..z6 (near nut -> elbow)
nodes.rationale: Fret-zones torso_z1..torso_z6 run along the torso near nut -> elbow, sites >= 4-5 cm apart to stay within torso two-point resolution (~35-40 mm); 12 musical frets map onto 6 torso zones via zone + intensity.

nodes.label: ALSA logical-channel routing (card, channel -> jack -> amp -> actuator)
nodes.rationale: Each Vantec exposes its 8 channels as one card; renderer routes logical channel index -> (card, channel) -> jack -> amp -> actuator per config/channel_map.json. Keep wiring table and that file in sync. Label channel number on both ends of every speaker-wire run and on each amp; a mislabeled channel is a silent bug.
nodes.norm_label: alsa logical-channel routing (card, channel -> jack -> amp -> actuator)

nodes.label: Wire weight problem (18 AWG levers joints)
nodes.norm_label: wire weight problem (18 awg levers joints)

nodes.label: 2-axis code (string -> back actuator + fret -> torso zone + intensity)
nodes.rationale: A note = (string, fret) rendered as two simultaneous cues: STRING axis -> which back actuator (6, high E top -> low E bottom); FRET axis -> which torso zone + intensity (6 zones x 2 levels = 12 frets). A played note fires one back actuator + one fret-zone actuator together as a short pulse.
nodes.norm_label: 2-axis code (string -> back actuator + fret -> torso zone + intensity)

nodes.label: Fret -> torso zone + intensity table (12 frets on 6 zones)
nodes.rationale: 6 fret-zone actuators z1-z6 carry 12 frets via zone + intensity (2 levels): frets 1-2 zone1, 3-4 zone2, 5-6 zone3, 7-8 zone4, 9-10 zone5, 11-12 zone6 (low/high); open string = brief double-tap on string actuator only. Skin resolves ~4-8 intensity steps so 2 levels per zone is safe.
nodes.norm_label: fret -> torso zone + intensity table (12 frets on 6 zones)

nodes.label: Chord / strum rendering (sequential sweep vs gestalt)
nodes.rationale: Sequential (recommended): fire each note's string+fret pair in fast succession low->high string ~40 ms apart so it feels like a strum sweep and a wrong note feels like a wrong spot. Gestalt (optional): common chord shapes get one memorized pattern; chord root rides the corresponding back string actuator. Sequential firing also keeps peak power low (<=2-3 channels at once).
nodes.norm_label: chord / strum rendering (sequential sweep vs gestalt)

nodes.label: Encoding design rules (separate spatially, sharp pulses, structured map)
nodes.rationale: Design rules or it turns to mush: separate simultaneous activations spatially (back vs torso) to avoid funneling into a blob; sharp pulses not drones; structured mapping only (musical neighbors -> body neighbors); keep highest-resolution info (fret detail) on higher-acuity sites (torso over the broader back).
nodes.norm_label: encoding design rules (separate spatially, sharp pulses, structured map)

nodes.label: Audio branch (F0 / transcription, dynamics, technique model)
nodes.rationale: Python audio branch: F0/transcription (pYIN/YIN live, CREPE offline) -> notes + onsets + durations; dynamics RMS -> volume; technique/quality ML model -> {clean|buzz|muted|dead|ghost} from timbre (harmonic-to-noise ratio, inharmonicity, spectral centroid/flux, attack/decay envelope, broadband-buzz energy via librosa).
nodes.norm_label: audio branch (f0 / transcription, dynamics, technique model)

nodes.label: Vision branch (MediaPipe hand pose + ArUco fretboard homography)
nodes.rationale: Browser vision branch: MediaPipe 21 landmarks/hand -> fingertips + which finger; OpenCV + ArUco marker -> homography mapping fingertips to (string, fret) + placement vs fret-wire (coarse). Vision features stream browser -> Python over the localhost WebSocket, timestamped.
nodes.norm_label: vision branch (mediapipe hand pose + aruco fretboard homography)

nodes.label: Fusion (time-align + reconcile vision vs audio, cross-modal confidence)
nodes.rationale: Python fusion (rules + light ML): time-align audio onsets to vision frames; vision resolves WHICH string/fret made a pitch, audio confirms it sounded + quality. buzz + finger-too-far-from-wire -> placement fault; dead/weak + finger-present -> pressure fault; clean + matches target -> correct. Confidence = cross-modal agreement.
nodes.norm_label: fusion (time-align + reconcile vision vs audio, cross-modal confidence)

nodes.label: Reference diff + any-song engine
nodes.rationale: Song-agnostic engine. Free play: transcribe whatever is played -> render + map, no reference. Coach vs a song: auto-generate the target (fetch MIDI/tab or run basic-pitch on the original recording), then diff discrepancies on note | fret/finger choice | timing | dynamics | technique. New song = no code change.

nodes.label: LLM coach (Anthropic, phrase-level feedback)
nodes.rationale: Two timescales: per-note real-time deterministic haptic flag + screen highlight (LLM too slow per-note); per-phrase LLM coach (Anthropic SDK) turns a structured error log into prioritized encouraging advice. Optional Redis for per-user mistake history, adaptive difficulty, vector-search of past mistakes.

nodes.label: Assembly checklist + bring-up (staged go/no-go gates)
nodes.rationale: Build in staged order with a go/no-go gate per stage; biggest risk is build time, so prove a small slice end-to-end before scaling.

nodes.label: Stage 1 — prove audio path before any soldering (speaker-test -c8)
nodes.rationale: speaker-test -c8 confirms all 8 channels map to the 4 jacks per Vantec; do not solder until every Vantec maps cleanly; fall back to a Sabrent dongle for a misbehaving jack.
nodes.norm_label: stage 1 — prove audio path before any soldering (speaker-test -c8)

nodes.label: Stage 2 — gut + tone-test each amp (12 channels buzz)
nodes.rationale: Gut each SK473 keeping its 2 KHD drivers, solder each on its own isolated 2-wire pair, tone-test so all 12 channels buzz; replace dead boards from the spare SK473.
nodes.norm_label: stage 2 — gut + tone-test each amp (12 channels buzz)

nodes.label: Stage 4 — chord stress test (fire all 12 at once)
nodes.rationale: Firing all 12 at felt gain is a worst-case stress check; real playback is a sequential strum with <=2 drivers active (~2-3 W). Gate: no brown-out, no cut-outs.
nodes.norm_label: stage 4 — chord stress test (fire all 12 at once)

nodes.label: Stage 5 — mount + couple (6 strings on back, 6 fret-zones on torso)
nodes.rationale: Mount actuators 6 strings on back + 6 fret-zones on torso with VHB + rigid backer, service loops and garment anchors so no actuator bears wire weight; each site clearly felt and secure under arm movement.
nodes.norm_label: stage 5 — mount + couple (6 strings on back, 6 fret-zones on torso)

nodes.label: Stage 6 — software bring-up (channel map, renderer, AI engine, coach)
nodes.rationale: channel_map.json must match the wiring table; renderer plays a known (string, fret) to the correct body site; AI engine does webcam+mic transcription + vision overlay; coach loop end-to-end on one phrase.
nodes.norm_label: stage 6 — software bring-up (channel map, renderer, ai engine, coach)

nodes.label: Design Decisions (Why, and What We Rejected)
nodes.rationale: Records why each choice was made and what dead ends were rejected, so settled questions are not re-opened.
nodes.norm_label: design decisions (why, and what we rejected)

nodes.label: Decision: gut SK473 boards (PAM8403), one amp per 2 channels
nodes.rationale: Haptic actuators need only ~1 W/ch; small class-D amps are the right size, in stock, cheap. Rejected car amp (50-100 W overkill, needs 12 V), Pi DigiAMP+ (only 2 ch on shared I2S bus), MAX98357A (out of stock, would need channel-select resistors - which is why no resistor is in the audio path).
nodes.norm_label: decision: gut sk473 boards (pam8403), one amp per 2 channels

nodes.label: Decision: USB 7.1 cards (Vantec NBA-200U, CM6206), 2 of 3 used
nodes.rationale: V1 = ch 1-8, V2 = ch 9-12, V3 spare; one Vantec = 4 stereo jacks = 8 channels feeding 4 SK473; CM6206 does real 8-ch on Linux. Fallback: ~9 Sabrent AU-MMSA USB stereo dongles kept as per-channel fallback if a Vantec jack will not map.
nodes.norm_label: decision: usb 7.1 cards (vantec nba-200u, cm6206), 2 of 3 used

nodes.label: Decision: bass dropped (was Logitech Z313)
nodes.rationale: Once the project became note coaching, which note/string/fret matters, not chest-thump bass; a subwoofer needs its own enclosure + bigger amp. Rejected harvesting a Joy-Con/phone/controller transducer (those are LRA/ERM single-frequency buzzers needing a special haptic driver IC; only voice-coil exciters/shakers/woofers are audio-drivable).

nodes.label: Decision: encode note as location + timing, not frequency
nodes.rationale: A note = (string to back actuator) + (fret to torso zone + intensity) as a short ~160 Hz pulse (tune on-body; 200-250 Hz was a pre-teardown estimate). Skin cannot discriminate pitch (vibrotactile Weber ~25%; ~5-9 distinguishable buzz-pitches), so a one-fret frequency change is unfeelable as frequency; skin resolves location and timing well. Rejected mapping pitch to a speaker's vibration frequency.
nodes.norm_label: decision: encode note as location + timing, not frequency

nodes.label: Decision: brain is the laptop (M4 Pro 48 GB), Pi cut from critical path
nodes.rationale: A Pi 5 cannot run MediaPipe + transcription + LLM + 12-ch audio in real time, and the architecture put MediaPipe in the browser anyway; the laptop runs the full stack and drives the Vantecs. ESP32 is not in the audio path either.
nodes.norm_label: decision: brain is the laptop (m4 pro 48 gb), pi cut from critical path

nodes.label: Decision: Pressure inferred from timbre, no sensor
nodes.rationale: Detect pressure adequacy from audio (buzz/dead-note/clean) not a force sensor; the timbre signal is what matters for coaching and a learned model reads it. Framed honestly as pressure adequacy, not measured force. Rejected FSR-on-ESP32 (good for a v2 with real ground-truth, dropped to keep scope tight).
nodes.norm_label: decision: pressure inferred from timbre, no sensor

nodes.label: Perception References (Vibrotactile Findings)
nodes.rationale: Well-established vibrotactile findings grounding every encoding choice; the difference between guessing and designing.

nodes.label: Vibrotactile frequency: peak 200-300 Hz, Weber ~20-30% (poor pitch)
nodes.rationale: Useful tactile vibration ~10-1000 Hz, peak sensitivity ~200-300 Hz (Pacinian corpuscles); frequency discrimination poor (Weber ~20-30%), skin resolves maybe 5-9 distinct buzz pitches; a one-fret ~6% change is unfeelable as frequency. Tactus starting pulse 160 Hz.
nodes.norm_label: vibrotactile frequency: peak 200-300 hz, weber ~20-30% (poor pitch)

nodes.label: Two-point discrimination (fingertip 2-4mm, forearm 35-40mm, torso/back 40-50mm)
nodes.rationale: Cited generic anatomy (Weinstein-style): fingertip ~2-4mm, palm ~10mm, forearm ~35-40mm, torso/back ~40-50mm. Consequence: keep actuators >=4-5cm apart; put higher-resolution frets on torso, coarser strings on back.

nodes.label: Temporal acuity ~5ms gap detection (skin's strength) -> sharp pulses + sweep
nodes.rationale: Gap/successiveness detection ~5 ms; skin times far better than it pitches; use sharp pulses (30-80 ms) not drones; render strums as a timed sweep low to high so a wrong note feels like a wrong spot in the sweep.
nodes.norm_label: temporal acuity ~5ms gap detection (skin's strength) -> sharp pulses + sweep

nodes.label: Funneling / sensory saltation (separate simultaneous cues across regions)
nodes.rationale: Two near-simultaneous stimuli close together merge into one perceived locus; fire simultaneous cues on separated sites, never adjacent.
nodes.norm_label: funneling / sensory saltation (separate simultaneous cues across regions)

nodes.label: Neosensory Buzz (Eagleman) — 4 wrist actuators, learnable code
nodes.rationale: Encodes audio onto 4 wrist actuators; users learn to discriminate words. Lesson: few well-placed actuators + a structured learnable code beats a dumb pile.
nodes.norm_label: neosensory buzz (eagleman) — 4 wrist actuators, learnable code

nodes.label: Precedents: Model Human Cochlea, SoundShirt, Braille / the vOICe
nodes.rationale: Model Human Cochlea maps audio frequency bands spatially across the torso; SoundShirt does torso vibrotactile music for Deaf audiences; Braille / the vOICe prove structured sensory-substitution codes are learnable.
nodes.norm_label: precedents: model human cochlea, soundshirt, braille / the voice

nodes.label: Build refinements + the kinks that will bite
nodes.rationale: Sharpens docs 01-09/14; collects non-obvious failure modes in the as-purchased build with an exact fix for each, a tightened connector map, and a single-channel-first build order.
nodes.norm_label: build refinements + the kinks that will bite

nodes.label: PAM8403 is BTL — never common the '-' outputs (isolated pair per channel)
nodes.rationale: PAM8403 is filterless Class-D bridge-tied-load: both output terminals (+ and -) are actively driven, neither is ground. Never connect any output - to GND, never join two channels' - together; every actuator gets its own isolated 2-conductor pair. Keep + to + phase consistent. Meter each - to power GND; it must NOT read ~0 Ohm.
nodes.norm_label: pam8403 is btl — never common the '-' outputs (isolated pair per channel)

nodes.label: Vantec/ALSA enumeration trap (bind by USB port, verify channel order)
nodes.rationale: Three identical CM6206 cards enumerate as the same name and reorder hw:0/1/2 across reboots; bind by physical USB port (by-id/by-path or udev) stored in channel_map.json card_alsa, not bare 0/1/2. CM6206 channel order is FL,FR,FC,LFE,RL,RR,SL,SR (center/LFE before rears) - verify alsa_ch empirically with speaker-test before trusting the map. The center jack ch4 'LFE' passes full-band on a raw hw: device.
nodes.norm_label: vantec/alsa enumeration trap (bind by usb port, verify channel order)

nodes.label: Wire gauge: only 18 AWG zip-cord on hand (22 AWG BOM line stale)
nodes.rationale: Per Aditya the only wire on hand is AudioVox 18 AWG speaker zip-cord; the BOM's 22 AWG solid-core line is stale. It is heavy and tears joints (strain relief mandatory) and too fat for PAM8403 pads (fan/trim strands, tin first, hot-glue blob carries load). Best cheap upgrade is 24-26 AWG stranded silicone. For the Mode B power bus 18 AWG is correct (~2.85 A peak/bus).
nodes.norm_label: wire gauge: only 18 awg zip-cord on hand (22 awg bom line stale)

nodes.label: 40mm speakers are not exciters — coupling (contact button + backer + preload) is make-or-break
nodes.rationale: A cone speaker throws sound into air; pressed naively it is heard, barely felt. Bond a contact button to the cone centre and anchor the magnet/frame to a rigid backer (actuator_puck), firm preload lowers detection threshold, foam-isolate the backer to avoid crosstalk, keep sites >=4-5cm apart, optional cup seal kills air radiation. Saturday's #1 risk is coupling.
nodes.norm_label: 40mm speakers are not exciters — coupling (contact button + backer + preload) is make-or-break

nodes.label: Mode A power correction: hub is a buck'd 12V brick, run at felt level
nodes.rationale: The wall hub is not a dumb 5 V rail; the brick is ~12 V 4-5 A bucked to 5 V (~8-10 A at 5 V), clearing the 6.65 A hard-chord peak only if no low per-port limit (USB-3 default 0.9 A; charging hubs 1.5-2.4 A). At felt play the rig draws ~3.5 A / 18 W with big margin; spread leads across non-adjacent ports if a port limits. CC resistor on cut USB-C feeds is 5.1 kOhm.
nodes.norm_label: mode a power correction: hub is a buck'd 12v brick, run at felt level

nodes.label: Tightened connector list (3 rails: line-level / BTL drive / 5V power)
nodes.rationale: Three rails: audio is line-level on 3.5 mm, actuator drive is bare 2-wire BTL from the amp, 5 V power is a third independent thing reaching only the amps; one shared ground via the 3.5 mm sleeves. Never connect amp 5V to any 3.5mm pin, actuator wire to a Vantec, or one channel's - to another's anything. Scope: 14 soldered actuator pairs (28 joints) + 7 power taps + (Mode B) 3 bus feeds.
nodes.norm_label: tightened connector list (3 rails: line-level / btl drive / 5v power)

nodes.label: Single-channel-first build order (prove one full channel, then mass-produce)
nodes.rationale: Gut ONE SK473, solder ONE actuator (pre-load heat-shrink), tone-test at 230 Hz and feel it on a puck+strap (the real M1), only then gut the other 6 and solder the remaining 13 actuators tone-testing each amp before gluing; hot-glue only boards that passed; label every wire both ends; build the 5 V buses last with CC resistor.
nodes.norm_label: single-channel-first build order (prove one full channel, then mass-produce)

nodes.label: SK473 teardown (Jun 20): 6 stereo PAIRS = 12 channels, amp is separable
nodes.rationale: Each SK473 box is a 2.0 set: one stereo PAM8403 board in the master speaker + a passive slave on a 2-wire cable; per box = 1 board (2 ch) + 2 drivers. 6 boxes x 2 = 12 channels (map to 6 back strings + 6 torso fret-zones, drop reserved ch 13-14). The driver-to-board joints are 2 snippable joints, not a weld; open only the 6 master speakers. Do NOT strap boxed speakers to the body (radiate into air, ~4 kg bulk).
nodes.norm_label: sk473 teardown (jun 20): 6 stereo pairs = 12 channels, amp is separable

nodes.label: Switch body actuators to SK473's own 12x KHD 3 Ohm/5 W drivers (de-housed)
nodes.rationale: The SK473's built-in KHD 3 Ohm/5 W drivers are bigger/better than the bought 40 mm and factory-matched to the amp (safest load); de-house them so the bare cone couples to skin (40 mm become spares). 3 Ohm is below the 4 Ohm nominal min but is the factory pairing run at felt level (~1 W) with <=2 channels active. Recommended: amps in the box, splice-extend driver red/black with 18 AWG to the vest. Volume pots ignored/bypassed; set level per channel in software. Measure driver dia and set spk_dia in actuator_puck.scad.
nodes.norm_label: switch body actuators to sk473's own 12x khd 3 ohm/5 w drivers (de-housed)

nodes.label: Tuning & Calibration Experiments (on-body)
nodes.rationale: Experiment plan + results table to find the SK473 KHD drivers' best drive parameters on the body: optimal drive frequency, optimal pressure, how punchy. The winners become the renderer defaults.

nodes.label: The 4 tuning knobs (drive freq, pulse shape, coupling pressure, per-channel gain)
nodes.rationale: Drive frequency (heavy 3 Ohm drivers often feel strongest LOWER ~80-160 Hz despite Pacinian peak ~250 Hz, start 150-200 Hz), pulse shape (50 ms sharp attack = punchy), coupling pressure (snug strap + contact button lowers threshold), per-channel gain in software (the real volume control, not the pots).
nodes.norm_label: the 4 tuning knobs (drive freq, pulse shape, coupling pressure, per-channel gain)

nodes.label: Experiment 1 — drive-frequency sweep per zone (back vs torso 60/100/150/200/250 Hz)
nodes.rationale: Mount one driver+puck on a back site (firm strap), play 500 ms tones at 60/100/150/200/250 Hz, rate felt strength 0-5 and localization, pick strongest+cleanest, repeat on a torso site (back and torso may prefer different freqs). Do this FIRST.
nodes.norm_label: experiment 1 — drive-frequency sweep per zone (back vs torso 60/100/150/200/250 hz)

nodes.label: Experiment 2 — punchiness (pulse shape 30/50/80 ms, flat vs sharp-decay)
nodes.rationale: At the chosen freq, play 30/50/80 ms pulses with flat envelope and sharp-attack to fast-decay; rate punch and distinctness (can you tell two quick taps apart).
nodes.norm_label: experiment 2 — punchiness (pulse shape 30/50/80 ms, flat vs sharp-decay)

nodes.label: Experiment 3 — coupling pressure (loose/snug/tight, with/without contact button)
nodes.rationale: Loose vs snug vs tight strap, with vs without the contact button on the cone; rate felt strength, localization, comfort; pick the firmest that is still comfortable.
nodes.norm_label: experiment 3 — coupling pressure (loose/snug/tight, with/without contact button)

nodes.label: Experiment 4 — per-channel gain calibration (equal feel across 12 sites)
nodes.rationale: Play the chosen pulse on every site at equal digital gain, rate relative strength, set a per-channel gain so all 12 feel equal, store in config/ (e.g. encoding.json); the 60-sec per-wearer staircase (docs/12) layers on top.
nodes.norm_label: experiment 4 — per-channel gain calibration (equal feel across 12 sites)

nodes.label: Tuning safety bounds (never clip; surface <40C; felt level only)
nodes.rationale: Never drive to clipping (a clipped Class-D output is near-DC into the coil, cooks it and feels buzzy); keep driver surface <~40 C (5 W ceiling, ~1 W used); <=2 channels active at once makes power a non-issue but per channel stay at felt level not max.
nodes.norm_label: tuning safety bounds (never clip; surface <40c; felt level only)

nodes.label: Chord, Sustain & Percussive Rendering
nodes.rationale: DRAFT extending the single-note encoder (docs/07) to chords, sustain, strums, percussive hits on the 12-ch as-built rig. Governing principle: render the actual per-string onset + amplitude envelope detected, never a chord symbol, so the many ways to play a chord are handled for free as an honest signal-to-skin transform.

nodes.label: Chord onset encodes how it was struck (strum sweep / pluck bloom / arpeggio)
nodes.rationale: Strum = a spread sequential sweep across the string-column (down-strum low-E to high-E, up reversed, faster sweep = harder); simultaneous fingerpick/block chord = a tight bloom within ~15 ms; arpeggio is already sequential. A wrong note lands as a wrong spot in the sweep.
nodes.norm_label: chord onset encodes how it was struck (strum sweep / pluck bloom / arpeggio)

nodes.label: Sustain = envelope-modulated looped shimmer (LOCKED)
nodes.rationale: A held/ringing chord is re-triggered shimmer not a static hold: cycle through ringing strings at a fast stagger so each is re-triggered before it perceptually fades, the whole shimmer's amplitude follows the chord's real decay envelope, stopping when energy decays below threshold. Re-trigger because (1) adaptation desensitizes sustained fixed-site vibration (~10 dB fade), (2) simultaneous neighbors mask each other, (3) apparent motion / Tactile Brush (Israr & Poupyrev) makes discrete taps read as continuous ringing.

nodes.label: Fret-zones carry chord SHAPE (pulsed in sync) + root emphasis
nodes.rationale: Fret-zones carry the chord shape (which frets) on the torso, pulsed in sync with the shimmer (not held, so they don't add steady channels or mask); the lowest/root string gets slightly stronger / more frequent re-trigger so the chord has a felt foundation.
nodes.norm_label: fret-zones carry chord shape (pulsed in sync) + root emphasis

nodes.label: Percussive & muted hits (sharp pop across motors; muted strings don't fire)
nodes.rationale: A percussive chunk / palm-muted strum / string slap is a quick simultaneous pop across all motors (a single sharp ~15 ms transient); dead/muted strings simply don't fire their string-speaker so the felt gap is itself information (you muted the G).
nodes.norm_label: percussive & muted hits (sharp pop across motors; muted strings don't fire)

nodes.label: Renderer input: per-string (onset, pitch->string/fret, envelope, decay) stream
nodes.rationale: Per active string a stream of (onset time, pitch->string/fret, amplitude envelope, offset/decay) from the engine (mic transcription + vision) for live play or read from the reference (tab/MIDI) for the target; the renderer turns it into onset-type (sweep vs bloom) -> looped shimmer (envelope-modulated) -> fret-zone shape pulses -> stop at decay threshold.
nodes.norm_label: renderer input: per-string (onset, pitch->string/fret, envelope, decay) stream

nodes.label: GSTACK eng-review report (PAUSED; B1-B7 open, 3 critical silent-failure gates)
nodes.rationale: /plan-eng-review PAUSED mid-review. Decisions locked: spine-first sequencing, two-path renderer (live rhythm-pop on onset-detect then located buzz on settle; reference pre-scheduled), sqrt(N) simultaneous-onset amplitude clamp + Mode A + metered Stage-4 pop check. Top P1 open items A3/A5/B1-B7; 3 CRITICAL silent-failure gates (channel-order map, 12-ch pop current draw, callback starvation under AR+MediaPipe load). VERDICT: NOT CLEARED.
nodes.norm_label: gstack eng-review report (paused; b1-b7 open, 3 critical silent-failure gates)

nodes.label: Interface: Live AR + 2D Correction (the centerpiece)
nodes.rationale: DRAFT god-tier interactive experience, supersedes the flat sheet-left/camera-right layout in docs/16; keeps docs/16's law: every pixel is a sense the Deaf learner does not have (cool == useful). Two coupled views: live-AR Beat-Saber play on the real neck + a dead-simple 2D fretboard showing what each finger did wrong.

nodes.label: Live AR play — Beat-Saber depth-approach on the real fretboard
nodes.rationale: Notes fly in from depth (Z axis) and land on the exact (string, fret) cell on the beat, arriving where the finger goes (killing the chart-to-hand translation). Registration via ArUco on the headstock (+ optional body marker) to per-frame 6-DoF neck pose, temporally smoothed (one-euro/Kalman), rendered in the neck coordinate frame. Color = per-string (6 colors) same palette as the back body-zones. Sustain = a depth tail; chord = notes together (bloom) or strum sweep.
nodes.norm_label: live ar play — beat-saber depth-approach on the real fretboard

nodes.label: Virtual-neck fallback (demo insurance — build FIRST)
nodes.rationale: A virtual-neck mirror: the identical Z-axis renderer with a swappable backdrop (live camera+ArUco when pose is solid, a clean rendered neck when it isn't). Same experience, only backdrop + pose source change; jittery AR looks worse than none, so the centerpiece can never die on stage.

nodes.label: Targeted 2D correction fretboard (what you played vs target ghost, animated fix)
nodes.rationale: On a miss, rewind the segment and switch to a top-down 2D fretboard schematic: per finger show what you played (string, fret, sub-fret distance d, pressure dot hollow/solid/over-saturated) + the target as ghost dots + dead-simple animated arrows / press-down pulses in ASL-gloss-friendly language. The correction view IS the docs/20 fusion output, drawn.
nodes.norm_label: targeted 2d correction fretboard (what you played vs target ghost, animated fix)

nodes.label: Per-finger placement (solid, vision) + pressure (vision-led, audio cross-checked)
nodes.rationale: Per-finger placement (which finger, where, wire-distance d) is solid from vision; per-finger pressure is a vision-led estimate (finger-load geometry) cross-checked by the global audio buzz - robust for single notes, best-effort attribution in chords; show a hint not a fake precise number.
nodes.norm_label: per-finger placement (solid, vision) + pressure (vision-led, audio cross-checked)

nodes.label: One phase-locked signal coherence (screen + body + sound from one stream)
nodes.rationale: The AR notes, the body-aurora ambient, and the haptic drive all come from one stream; nail a note and all channels confirm together - the alive, not an app feeling.
nodes.norm_label: one phase-locked signal coherence (screen + body + sound from one stream)

nodes.label: actuator_puck.scad — Ø52 driver coupling puck (cup + button)
nodes.rationale: Renders the actuator coupling puck for the Ø52 mm SK473 KHD cone driver (Havit HV-SK473, web-verified 'Φ52mm*2'). spk_dia was just set to 52.0 (replacing the earlier 40/58 mm placeholder); caliper still to confirm cone-vs-frame. Turns an air-radiating cone into a skin tactor: rigid cup kills air radiation, domed button pokes the dust cap into skin. part='both' renders cup() + button() side by side. Headless render via _puck_render.py (manifold3d) since OpenSCAD is x86 and won't run on arm64 Mac.
nodes.norm_label: actuator_puck.scad — ø52 driver coupling puck (cup + button)

nodes.label: cup() — rigid coupling cup that grips the Ø52 driver frame
nodes.rationale: Closed-back cup sized from Ø52 driver: cup_id = spk_dia(52.0) + spk_fit_gap(0.6) = 52.6 mm bore, cup_od = cup_id + 2*wall(2.4). The 0.6 mm radial clearance lets the speaker drop in and is PETG-shrink-safe; rim_lip 2.0 mm retains the rim. Includes wire-exit notch (6 mm wide for 18 AWG zip-cord), strain-relief zip-tie hole (offloads cable from speaker tabs), two zip/sew ears, and a small Ø4 back vent so the trapped cone air can breathe.
nodes.norm_label: cup() — rigid coupling cup that grips the ø52 driver frame

nodes.label: button() — domed contact button bonded to the dust cap
nodes.rationale: Ø14 mm flat-base (1.6 mm) + shallow dome (4.5 mm proud). Glued/VHB'd to the centre of the driver dust cap so it stands ~2-3 mm proud of the cup rim and hits skin first, transferring cone excursion directly into the body.
nodes.norm_label: button() — domed contact button bonded to the dust cap

nodes.label: tactus_box.scad — main electronics enclosure (brain-pack)
nodes.rationale: Holds 3x Vantec NBA-200U USB 7.1 cards, 7x gutted SK473/PAM8403 amp bricks, optional Pi 5. Dimension-tolerant (VHB foam + zip-ties via floor slot grid, not press-fit, since part dims are estimated). Strain-relief comb wall takes 14-wire umbilical load off amp pads (#1 failure mode). Vented (floor+wall+lid). Outer kept <~210mm for safe FlashForge Adventurer 5M 220 bed. Pi cut from scope (laptop-only) by default. Print base + lid separately.

nodes.label: rprism(x,y,h,r) — rounded-rectangle prism
nodes.rationale: Rounded vertical corners (corner_r=7) so the shell reads as a designed product unit, not a raw cube. Shared by base() outer shell and lid() top plate.

nodes.label: logo_cut() — engraved TACTUS text on lid
nodes.rationale: Engraves TACTUS into the lid plate for product look.
nodes.norm_label: logo_cut() — engraved tactus text on lid

nodes.label: base() — enclosure base shell + mounts + vents
nodes.rationale: Top-level base part: rounded shell + comb ledge, main open-top cavity, umbilical pass-throughs, laptop-side glands, floor/short-wall vents, belt strap slots, zip-tie floor grid; adds Pi standoffs (optional), lid bosses, cable anchors AFTER the difference so the cavity doesn't eat them.
nodes.norm_label: base() — enclosure base shell + mounts + vents

nodes.label: comb_ledge() — external strain-relief comb on +Y wall
nodes.rationale: External ledge on +Y wall; holes/slots cut later in wire_passthroughs(). Part of the strain-relief comb that takes umbilical load off amp output pads.
nodes.norm_label: comb_ledge() — external strain-relief comb on +y wall

nodes.label: wire_passthroughs() — umbilical holes + open-top comb slots
nodes.rationale: Row of Ø7 holes through +Y wall and comb ledge with open-top slots so a wire can be pressed in from above (no threading). n_wires=16 (14 used + 2 spare).
nodes.norm_label: wire_passthroughs() — umbilical holes + open-top comb slots

nodes.label: gland_holes() — laptop-side USB + power glands (-Y wall)
nodes.rationale: -Y wall: 3 USB cable glands (Ø13) for the Vantec USB cables to the laptop + 1 power-feed gland (Ø10).
nodes.norm_label: gland_holes() — laptop-side usb + power glands (-y wall)

nodes.label: floor_vents() — floor slot grid for airflow
nodes.rationale: Grid of floor slots for airflow under the boards + Pi (amps run warm).
nodes.norm_label: floor_vents() — floor slot grid for airflow

nodes.label: shortwall_vents() — vertical slots in short walls
nodes.rationale: Vertical vent slots in the two short walls (x=0 and x=outer_x) for cross-flow cooling.
nodes.norm_label: shortwall_vents() — vertical slots in short walls

nodes.label: strap_slots() — belt/strap slots in floor (-Y edge)
nodes.rationale: Two floor slots near the -Y edge so a belt/strap can be threaded to wear the pack.
nodes.norm_label: strap_slots() — belt/strap slots in floor (-y edge)

nodes.label: ziptie_floor_grid() — paired zip-tie slots to lash boards
nodes.rationale: Pairs of thin slots (a zip tie loops up one, over the board, down the other) so any board can be mounted anywhere — dimension-tolerant alternative to press-fit pockets.
nodes.norm_label: ziptie_floor_grid() — paired zip-tie slots to lash boards

nodes.label: pi_standoffs() — Raspberry Pi 5 mount bosses
nodes.rationale: Standoffs on the Pi 5 58x49 hole pattern with Ø2.3 M2.5 self-tap bores. Cut from scope by default (include_pi=false) to free floor for amps/Vantecs.

nodes.label: lid_bosses() — corner screw bosses (base side)
nodes.rationale: Tall corner posts at boss_pts with Ø2.6 M3 self-tap bores; boss_pts shared by base + lid so holes align.

nodes.label: cable_anchors() — internal zip-tie posts at wire wall
nodes.rationale: Two internal posts just inside the wire wall to lash the internal bundle to (both-end strain relief together with the external comb).
nodes.norm_label: cable_anchors() — internal zip-tie posts at wire wall

nodes.label: lid() — enclosure lid plate
nodes.rationale: Top-level lid part: rounded top plate + nesting lip (~0.8mm/side loose fit locates lid into cavity), engraved logo, corner clearance + countersink screw holes aligned to boss_pts, lid vent slot array.

nodes.label: tactus_chest_plate.scad — body-contoured plate holding 6 string drivers
nodes.rationale: Cylindrical-section torso shell carrying a vertical column of 6 self-adjusting driver sockets (high-E top to low-E bottom). drv_dia was just set to 52 (Ø52 web-verified, Havit HV-SK473) replacing the placeholder; depth(27)/magnet(30) and torso_r(150)/col_pitch(46) remain PLACEHOLDERS until the wearer + vest are measured, then re-render in OpenSCAD. Prints as 2 tiles to fit the bed and flex to the body. use <tactus_socket.scad> for socket geometry.
nodes.norm_label: tactus_chest_plate.scad — body-contoured plate holding 6 string drivers

nodes.label: shell() — curved torso-cylinder shell panel between z_lo..z_hi
nodes.rationale: A front section of the torso cylinder: difference of two cylinders (torso_r + plate_t outer, torso_r inner) intersected with an arc_half-wide chord box. Modeled from z=0 and translated by tile().
nodes.norm_label: shell() — curved torso-cylinder shell panel between z_lo..z_hi

nodes.label: socket_at(z) — places a driver_socket on the inner face at height z
nodes.rationale: Positions a shared driver_socket(drv_dia=52, drv_depth, drv_mag_dia) on the front inner face, skin-side toward the body. The Ø52 fit is delegated to tactus_socket.scad's driver_socket().
nodes.norm_label: socket_at(z) — places a driver_socket on the inner face at height z

nodes.label: bore_at(z) — through-hole so the cone/button reaches skin
nodes.rationale: Ø(drv_dia - 8) = Ø44 through-bore in the panel at height z so the cone/contact button protrudes toward the body.
nodes.norm_label: bore_at(z) — through-hole so the cone/button reaches skin

nodes.label: strap_slots(z_lo, z_hi) — edge slots to lash onto the laser-tag vest
nodes.rationale: Cuts strap slots along the top and bottom edges (3 across each) for lashing the plate to the laser-tag vest chest panel.
nodes.norm_label: strap_slots(z_lo, z_hi) — edge slots to lash onto the laser-tag vest

nodes.label: tile(z_lo, z_hi) — one print tile (shell + sockets - bores - slots)
nodes.rationale: Builds one tile by unioning the translated shell with the sockets whose z falls in range, then subtracting bores and strap slots. Uses translated shell() (relative-z modeling); tile_abs() is the absolute-coordinate alternative.
nodes.norm_label: tile(z_lo, z_hi) — one print tile (shell + sockets - bores - slots)

nodes.label: tile_abs(z_lo, z_hi) — print tile built at absolute z coordinates
nodes.rationale: The actually-rendered tile builder: inlines the shell geometry at absolute z (rather than translating shell()) so sockets land at correct absolute heights, then subtracts bore_at and strap_slots. Driven by part = full | top | bottom.
nodes.norm_label: tile_abs(z_lo, z_hi) — print tile built at absolute z coordinates

nodes.label: tactus_node_mount.scad — per-node split-clamp + telescoping driver mount
nodes.rationale: Per-node adjustable speaker mount: a split-clamp collar (base) + telescoping socket-barrel (GoPro/tube-clamp style) for continuous depth/preload tuning, locked by one M3 screw. drv_dia was just set to 52 (Ø52 web-verified, Havit HV-SK473) replacing the placeholder; depth(27)/magnet(30) still to caliper-confirm and re-render. Print 1 base + 1 barrel per node (x12). use <tactus_socket.scad>.
nodes.norm_label: tactus_node_mount.scad — per-node split-clamp + telescoping driver mount

nodes.label: barrel() — telescoping sleeve carrying the driver socket
nodes.rationale: Sleeve (bar_od = socket_outer_d(52) + 5) that slides in the collar for depth adjust, with a finned grip ring at the outer end and the shared driver_socket(52, drv_depth, drv_mag_dia) dropped in at the body (skin) end. Slide finds the optimal-preload sweet spot.
nodes.norm_label: barrel() — telescoping sleeve carrying the driver socket

nodes.label: base() — split-clamp collar + vest-mount foot
nodes.rationale: Split collar (collar_od derived from bore_id = bar_od + 0.8 slide clearance) with a +X slit so an M3 clamp screw (with nut trap) squeezes it to lock the barrel at any depth, plus a -X foot flange with zip-tie slots / velcro pocket to mount on the vest.

nodes.label: tactus_power_cradle.scad — Anker 737 / USB hub power cradle
nodes.rationale: Open-faced vented sled, separate from the main box so a 140W Li-ion bank dumping ~33W vents and stays removable, and you can swap Anker 737 (Mode B cordless) <-> 10-port hub (Mode A wall) per demo while seeing the Anker screen + reaching its ports/button. Anker 737 (A1289) measured-spec 155.7 x 54.6 x 49.5 mm. Print one, PETG no supports.
nodes.norm_label: tactus_power_cradle.scad — anker 737 / usb hub power cradle

nodes.label: cradle() — vented open-faced power-bank sled
nodes.rationale: Pocket = device + clr(1.5) all-round clearance. Open-top so screen/ports face up; +X end fully open for Anker ports + button. Big floor vents + side-wall vents (heat off the bank). Velcro/strap slots through the floor. Finger scoop on -X end to pop the bank out. Two top retention lips so the device can't slide out.

nodes.label: tactus_socket.scad — self-adjusting driver socket (coupling-physics core)
nodes.rationale: Solves the optimal-pressure / goes-invisible problem: too loose -> cone moves air (heard not felt); too tight -> cone clamped (also invisible); sweet spot -> moderate floating preload. Socket grips the driver precisely (rigid mm-tolerance bore) while the skin side couples through a compliant foam/TPU pad so the contact button floats to the sweet-spot preload across a range of fits. Rigid where it meets the speaker, springy where it meets the body. Shared via use <tactus_socket.scad> by the chest plate and node mount.

nodes.label: driver_socket(dia,depth,mag_dia,fit,foam,rim,wall) — parametric grip socket
nodes.rationale: z=0 = skin side; driver loads from the back, cone faces out. socket_id = dia + fit(0.6) precise grip bore. socket_h = depth + foam(6) + rim(2.2). Front rim aperture lets cone/button poke through to the body. Back well (mag_dia+2) clears magnet and seats a compliant foam disc. Wire-exit slot sized for 18 AWG zip-cord. 3 grip-relief slots at 0/120/240 so the bore flexes a hair onto the driver. Reused by chest plate socket_at() and node-mount barrel().

nodes.label: contact_button(btn_dia,proud) — skin-contact dome
nodes.rationale: Glue base (1.6mm) bonds to the driver dust cap; skin dome (proud=2.5) stands proud so it floats into the body at the sweet-spot preload. Printed standalone (x12+) since the barrel already contains the socket.

nodes.label: socket_outer_d(dia,fit,wall) — socket OD helper function
nodes.rationale: Returns dia + fit + 2*wall so plates/barrels can space and size around sockets correctly. Consumed cross-file by chest plate (sock_od) and node mount (sock_od, bar_od).
